item.title: 裁判、事件和逃生舱口 | Refs, Events and Escape Hatches
item.description:
我希望在阅读了关于React 中过时的闭包的第一部分之后，您现在对它们为什么会发生以及如何避免它们有一个很好的理解。

但是，有时在传递函数时必须考虑引用稳定性是很麻烦的，而且所有这些额外的useCallbacks也会变得有点样板。

我已经在上一篇文章中留下了一堆线索，可能有办法让这更容易，所以我们开始吧：

还记得这句话吗？

关于图片的事情是 - 它不能改变。 一旦我们拍摄了它，它的内容就会被密封（除非我们使用 Photoshop）。

在 React 中，我们几乎总是考虑不变性。 道具不能被改变（从一个组件内），状态更新只是被安排并且也需要以不可变的方式发生。 我们看到和使用的所有东西本质上都是一个const 。

我们总是从它们被创建的那一刻起就看到它们，但是如果它们在适当的位置发生了变异，我们仍然会看到最新的值。 让我们以第 1 部分中的基本示例为例，并将值存储在 React 组件外部的可变变量中：（注意：示例在我的博客上是交互式的： https ://tkdodo.eu/blog/refs-events-and-escape-hatches )

 let count = 1 function App () { const rerender = React . useReducer (() => ({}))[ 1 ] // 😱 logCount is stable, but it still sees // the latest value of count const logCount = React . useCallback (() => { console . log ( count ) }, []) return ( < div > < div > count is { count } < /div > < button onClick = {() => { count ++ rerender () }} > increment < /button > < button onClick = { logCount } > log < /button > < /div > ) }

现在很明显，这不是您使用 React 的方式。 在组件之外有一个变量意味着它在组件实例之间共享，而 React 不知道它，这就是为什么我们必须手动强制我们的组件重新渲染。

但它展示了一件事：在适当的位置改变一个变量绕过了我们无法改变的图片的心理模式。

突变实际上是 Photoshop。 他们可以在拍摄后更改现有图像。 保持可变值的 React 方法是什么？ 参考！

参考文献

如果我们必须传递函数，例如自定义钩子，这会非常方便。

例如，让我们实现一个比较常见的用例，即存储一个值并在一段时间后触发回调：

 export const useDebouncedState = ( callback , delay ) => { const [ value , setValue ] = React . useState ( '' ) React . useEffect (() => { const timeoutId = setTimeout (() => { if ( value ) { callback ( value ) } }, delay ) return () => { clearTimeout ( timeoutId ) } }, [ value , delay , callback ]) return [ value , setValue ] }

我们已经根据 linter 的需要为效果的依赖数组添加了回调，但这有一个缺点：消费者需要记忆函数，这不是一个对开发人员非常友好的接口。 当然，他们通常只想使用内联函数，而不必关心引用稳定性：

 const [ state , setState ] = useDebouncedState (( value ) => { alert ( value ) }, 1000 )

这可能会过于频繁地运行我们的效果，例如，如果组件由于其他原因重新渲染。 但是，从依赖数组中省略该函数不是一种选择，因为它可能会引入陈旧的闭包。

最新参考

我首先在Kent C. Dodds的这篇文章中读到了一个名为latest ref的模式。 这个想法是将函数（我们的回调）存储在 ref 中，以便我们可以从依赖数组中省略它。 然后，我们需要做的就是确保在函数更改时更新 ref，我们可以通过额外的效果来做到这一点：

 export const useDebouncedState = ( callback , delay ) => { const [ value , setValue ] = React . useState ( '' ) // 👇 store callback in a ref const ref = React . useRef ( callback ) // 👇 update the ref when the callback changes React . useLayoutEffect (() => { ref . current = callback }, [ callback ]) React . useEffect (() => { const timeoutId = setTimeout (() => { if ( value ) { // 👇 use the ref instead of the callback ref . current ( value ) } }, delay ) return () => { clearTimeout ( timeoutId ) } // 👇 no need to include the callback }, [ value , delay ]) return [ value , setValue ] }

诚然，这确实增加了更多样板，但它得到了回报——尤其是对于库或可重用的钩子，因为钩子的使用者改进了开发人员的体验。 另外，请坚持住，因为 React 可能会在未来发布一个钩子来为我们做这件事：

使用事件

最近，React 团队在 RFC 上工作了一个关于这种行为的专用钩子，称为useEvent 。 这个想法是有一个类似于useCallback的钩子，除了它没有依赖数组，但仍然返回一个稳定的函数引用，而不会遇到过时的闭包问题。

正如新的 beta 文档中所指出的，这些事件函数的行为更像一个事件处理程序（因此得名）。 它里面的逻辑不是反应式的，它总是能看到你的 props 和 state 的最新值。

如果该钩子已经存在，我们可以像这样重写我们的useDebouncedState钩子：

 export const useDebouncedState = ( callback , delay ) => { const [ value , setValue ] = React . useState ( '' ) // 👇 declare the event const onTimeout = React . useEvent ( callback ); React . useEffect (() => { const timeoutId = setTimeout (() => { if ( value ) { // 👇 use the event onTimeout ( value ); } }, delay ); return () => { clearTimeout ( timeoutId ); }; // 🎉 no need to include onTimeout }, [ value , delay ]); return [ value , setValue ] }

这对我来说看起来很干净，如果你现在想要一个用户级的useEvent实现，看看Diego Haz的这个实现：

你现在想在 TypeScript 中使用提议的 `useEvent` 钩子吗？

给你： gist.github.com/diegohaz/69509…

在 React 发布自己的钩子之前，您可以使用它，但请注意，您必须将返回的函数包含在依赖数组中，因为与本机实现不同，linter 无法知道该函数是稳定的。 我个人不能等到它发布，因为它会简化我的很多代码。 这就是今天的内容。 请随时在推特上与我联系
如果您有任何问题，或者只是在下面发表评论。 ⬇️ ⬇️



原文:

I hope that after reading part one about stale closures in React, you now have a good understanding of why they can occur and how to avoid them.

However, it is sometimes quite cumbersome to have to think about referential stability when passing functions around, and it can also get a bit boilerplate-y with all those additional useCallbacks.

I already left a bunch of clues in the previous article that there might be a way to make this easier, so here we go:

Photoshop

Remember this sentence?

The thing about the picture is - it cannot change. Once we have taken it, its content is sealed (unless we use photoshop).

In React, we almost always think in immutability. Props cannot be changed (from within a component), and state updates are merely scheduled and also need to happen in an immutable way. Everything we see and work with is essentially a const.

We always see the things from the time they were created, but if they are mutated in place, we would still see the latest value. Let's take the basic example from part 1 and store the value in a mutable variable outside the React component:(note: examples are interactive on my blog: https://tkdodo.eu/blog/refs-events-and-escape-hatches)

let count = 1
function App() { const rerender = React.useReducer(() => ({}))[1] // 😱 logCount is stable, but it still sees // the latest value of count const logCount = React.useCallback(() => { console.log(count) }, []) return ( <div> <div>count is {count}</div>
 <button onClick={() => { count++ rerender() }} > increment </button>
 <button onClick={logCount}>log</button>
 </div>
 )
}

Now it's hopefully obvious that this is not how you would work with React. Having a variable outside the component means it's shared between component instances, and React doesn't know about it, which is why we have to manually force our component to re-render.

But it showcases one thing: mutating a variable in place bypasses our mental modal of pictures that cannot change.

Mutations are literally photoshop. They can alter existing images after they have been taken. And what's the React way to hold mutable values? Refs!

Refs

Refs are just bags that can contain an arbitrary mutable value. The ref itself is referentially stable, so we don't have to include it in dependency arrays, and the .current property has the actual value. Refs offer an escape hatch from Reacts one way data flow model, because updates on refs are not going to make your component to re-render. This comes in quite handy if we have to pass functions around, e.g. to custom hooks.

As an example, let's implement a somewhat common use-case of storing a value and triggering a callback after some debounced time:

export const useDebouncedState = (callback, delay) => { const [value, setValue] = React.useState('') React.useEffect(() => { const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => { if (value) { callback(value) } }, delay) return () => { clearTimeout(timeoutId) } }, [value, delay, callback]) return [value, setValue]
}

We've added callback to the effect's dependency array as the linter wants, but that has a downside: consumers will need to memoize the function, which is not a very developer friendly interface. Of course, they'll most often just want to use an inline function, without having to care about referential stability:

const [state, setState] = useDebouncedState((value) => { alert(value)
}, 1000)

This will potentially run our effect too often, e.g. if the component re-renders for another reason. However, omitting the function from the dependency array is not an option, as it would potentially introduce stale closures.

The latest ref

I first read about a pattern called the latest ref in this article by Kent C. Dodds. The idea is to store the function (our callback) in a ref so that we can omit it from the dependency array. All we then need to do is to make sure that the ref is updated when the function changes, and we can do that with an additional effect:

export const useDebouncedState = (callback, delay) => { const [value, setValue] = React.useState('') // 👇 store callback in a ref const ref = React.useRef(callback) // 👇 update the ref when the callback changes React.useLayoutEffect(() => { ref.current = callback }, [callback]) React.useEffect(() => { const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => { if (value) { // 👇 use the ref instead of the callback ref.current(value) } }, delay) return () => { clearTimeout(timeoutId) } // 👇 no need to include the callback }, [value, delay]) return [value, setValue]
}

Admittedly, this does add more boilerplate, but it pays off - especially for libraries or reusable hooks due to the improved developer experience for consumers of the hook. Also, hold on tight because React might actually ship a hook that does this for us in the future:

useEvent

Recently, the React team has worked on an RFC about a dedicated hook for this behaviour called useEvent. The idea is to have a hook that is similar to useCallback, except that it doesn't have a dependency array, but still returns a stable function reference without suffering from stale closure problems.

As pointed out in the new beta docs, those event functions behave a lot more like an event handler (hence the name). The logic inside it is not reactive, and it always sees the latest values of your props and state.

Would that hook exist already, we could rewrite our useDebouncedState hook like this:

export const useDebouncedState = (callback, delay) => { const [value, setValue] = React.useState('') // 👇 declare the event const onTimeout = React.useEvent(callback); React.useEffect(() => { const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => { if (value) { // 👇 use the event onTimeout(value); } }, delay); return () => { clearTimeout(timeoutId); }; // 🎉 no need to include onTimeout }, [value, delay]); return [value, setValue]
}

This looks pretty clean to me, and if you want to have a user-land implementation of useEvent right now, have a look at this implementation by Diego Haz:

Do you want to use the proposed `useEvent` hook now with TypeScript?

Here you go: gist.github.com/diegohaz/69509…

You can use it until React ships their own hook, but be aware that you have to include the returned function in the dependency array because unlike the native implementation, the linter cannot know that the function is stable. I personally can't wait until this ships because it will simplify a lot of my code. 🙌

That's it for today. Feel free to reach out to me on twitter
if you have any questions, or just leave a comment below. ⬇️
item.author: tkdodo

item.title: 如何从终端（Mac 和 PC）合并/合并多个 CSV 或 Excel 文件 | How to Combine / Merge Multiple CSV or Excel Files from the Terminal (Mac & PC)
item.description:
德文舒马赫

发表于在“我们从事计算机工作”的行业中，我们一直在处理 CSV——组合、清理、分析、在你面前哭泣——你能说出它的名字。

因此，请允许我通过向您展示如何在几秒钟内组合 CSV，让您的生活更轻松。

在本文（和视频）中，我将向您展示如何使用终端快速轻松地合并和合并多个 CSV 文件，只使用终端即可。

所以继续并开始组合吧！

如何为 Mac 合并/合并多个 CSV/Excel 文件：

在您的桌面上创建一个新文件夹并将所有 CSV 文件放入其中。

我将把我的称为“组合”。

它将如下所示：

注意：此文件夹中的所有内容都将合并！

又名“我现在在我的文件系统中的什么位置？”

  1. 类型： pwd
  2. 按return键

将您的“目录”设置为您使用 CSV 初始化创建的文件夹。

  1. 类型： cd /Users/devin/Desktop/combine <== 显然用你的名字替换“devin”……除非你的名字是 devin。
  2. 按return键

您已准备好合并！

  1. 类型： cat *.csv >combined.csv
  2. 击return

如果你正确地跟随，你就完成了！

在您的文件夹中，您应该有一个名为“combined.csv”的文件，其中包含您所有的 csv……等待它……组合在一起。

注意：您可能需要删除重复的标题列！ 这些也结合了。

只需将过滤器应用于您的工作表，在“Col A”上对 AZ 进行排序，您应该会看到骗子。

想看更多？？

这很简单！ 成为 DEV 成员以关注此帖子、发表评论等。

布拉德利斯科菲尔德 - 一旦暂停，dvns 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，dvns 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dvns 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dvns 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 dvns 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 dvns 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Devin Schumacher

Posted on Sep 30

We're always dealing with CSVs here in the "we work on computers" industry - combining, cleaning, analyzing, crying in front of - you name it.

So, allow me to make your life just one byte easier by showing you how to combine CSVs in a matter of seconds.

In this article (and video), I'll show you how to use the terminal to quickly and easily combine and merge multiple CSV files using nothing but the terminal.

So go ahead and start combining!

How to combine/merge multiple CSV/Excel files for Mac:

00:00 Introduction
00:12 Step 1 – Create A New Folder
00:43 Step 2 – Open Terminal
01:08 Step 3 – Print Working Directory
01:41 Step 4 – Choose The Folder You Created
01:54 Step 5 – Merge The Files!
02:21 Step 6 – Check Your Folder

Step 1 – Create A New Folder

Make a new folder on your Desktop and place all of your CSV files in it.

I'm going to call mine "combine."

It will appear as follows:

Heads up: Everything in this folder will be combined!

Step 2 – Open Terminal

The “Terminal” is in your “Applications” folder under “Utilities” (Mac).

Step 3 – PWD

PWD stands for "Print Working Directory".

Aka "where am I right now in my filesystem?"

  1. Type: pwd
  2. Hit the return key

Step 4 – Set file path to your folder

Set your “directory” to the folder you created with your mess of CSVs init.

  1. type: cd /Users/devin/Desktop/combine <== obviously replace “devin” with your name… unless your name is devin.
  2. hit the return key

Step 5 – Merge Files

You're ready to merge!

  1. type: cat *.csv >combined.csv
  2. hit return

Step 6 – Check The Results!

If you followed along properly, you're done!

Inside your folder you should have a file called “combined.csv”, which has all your csvs ... wait for it ... combined together.

Note: you may need to delete duplicate header columns! Those got combined too.

Simply apply a filter to you sheet, sort A-Z on 'Col A' & you should see the dupes.

Want to see more??

🎥 Youtube
📸 Instagram
🐦 Twitter
👨‍💻 Website

--

🍻 Cheers & stay funky, my friends. 🦩

 
It's easy! Become a DEV member to follow this post, comment, and more.

Bradley Schofield - Sep 22

Once suspended, dvns will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, dvns will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dvns will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dvns is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag dvns:

dvns consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging dvns will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: dvns

item.title: 💻 Code-server：浏览器上很棒的 VS Code 容器（用于快速云部署 - 修复企业问题） | 💻 Code-server : Awesome VS Code container on browser (Useful for Fast Cloud Deploy - Fix Corporate Issues)
item.description:
我将介绍代码服务器的概念。

我既不是代码服务器的创建者，也不是开发者。

创世纪

由于一些限制（代理、防火墙、无能为力的机器、掌握的操作系统），我遇到了公司网络问题，因为我找到了开源代码服务器存储库。

概念

编码器发布

在任何地方的任何机器上运行VS Code并在浏览器中访问它。

强调

  • 在具有一致开发环境的任何设备上编写代码
  • 使用云服务器加速测试、编译、下载等
  • 在旅途中保持电池寿命； 所有密集型任务都在您的服务器上运行

要求

请参阅最低规格要求，以及有关如何设置可以安装代码服务器的 Google VM 的说明。

入门

有四种方法可以开始：

如果您使用安装脚本，您可以预览会发生什么……

实际上， Code-server是运行在远程服务器上的 VS Code，可以通过浏览器访问。

以下是主要优点：

  • 通过 Web UI 访问

  • 在具有一致开发环境的任何设备上编写代码。

  • 所有密集计算都在您的服务器上运行。

  • 在旅途中保持电池寿命。

  • 您不再运行多余的 Chrome 实例。

  • 轻松协作

您可以访问 coder 网站，该网站提供免费版本和 24/7h 支持的企业扩展版本，并提供云服务器自动部署 ( Coder )。

定期和及时的应用程序更新
  • 简单的用户映射（PGID、PUID）
  • 带有 s6 覆盖的自定义基础图像
  • 在整个 LinuxServer.io 生态系统中使用通用层每周更新基本操作系统，以最大限度地减少空间使用、停机时间和带宽
  • 定期安全更新

    • 在以下位置找到我们：

    • 博客- 您可以使用我们的容器做的所有事情，包括操作指南、意见等等！
    • 开放集体- 请考虑通过捐赠或贡献我们的预算来帮助我们

    以一致的方式在您的 Chromebook、平板电脑和笔记本电脑上编写代码……

    它提供了比编码器版本更多的灵活性和定制性。 它嵌入了带有一些层（Docker、Python、NodeJS、Java、PHP 等...-> docker-mods ）和一些默认包的 VS 代码。

    以下是主要特点：

    • 带有 S6 覆盖的自定义基础 Docker 映像（更多详细信息： just-containers/s6-overlay ）

    • 完全自定义和 docker 层 mods 集成 ( linuxserver/docker-mods )

    • 每周基本操作系统更新和定期安全更新

    • 简单的身份验证设置

    • 域代理

    • 这是层列表： https ://mods.linuxserver.io/?mod=code-server。

      代码服务器还有其他版本/存储库。

      要求（对于 linuxserver/code-server）

      • 码头工人
      • 至少 1 GB 的 RAM（2 或 4 GB 的 RAM 更好）
      • 最少 2 个 CPU 内核

      有关更多详细信息，请参阅： https ://github.com/coder/code-server/blob/main/docs/requirements.md

      安装（用于 linuxserver/code-server）

      这是 docker-compose.yml 的示例，然后您就可以开始创建容器了。

       --- version : " 2.1" services : code-server : image : linuxserver/code-server:latest container_name : code-server environment : - PUID=1000 - PGID=1000 - TZ=Europe/London - PASSWORD=password #optional - HASHED_PASSWORD= #optional - SUDO_PASSWORD=password #optional - SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH= #optional - PROXY_DOMAIN=code-server.my.domain #optional - DEFAULT_WORKSPACE=/config/workspace #optional - DOCKER_MODS=linuxserver/mods:python #example volumes : - /path/to/appdata/config:/config ports : - 8443:8443 restart : unless-stopped
      范围功能
      -p 8443 网页界面
      -e PUID=1000 对于用户 ID - 请参阅下面的说明
      -e PGID=1000 对于 GroupID - 请参阅下面的说明
      -e TZ=Europe/London 指定时区以使用 EG Europe/London
      -e PASSWORD=password 可选的 web gui 密码，如果没有提供PASSWORD或HASHED_PASSWORD ，将没有 auth。
      -e HASHED_PASSWORD= 可选的 web gui 密码，覆盖PASSWORD ，如何创建它的说明如下。
      -e SUDO_PASSWORD=password 如果设置了此可选变量，用户将使用指定的密码在代码服务器终端中进行 sudo 访问。
      -e SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH= 可选择通过哈希设置 sudo 密码（优先于SUDO_PASSWORD var）。 格式为$type$salt$hashed 。
      -e PROXY_DOMAIN=code-server.my.domain 如果设置了此可选变量，则该域将被代理以进行子域代理。 查看文档
      -e DEFAULT_WORKSPACE=/config/workspace 如果设置了这个可选变量，code-server 默认会打开这个目录
      -v /config 包含所有相关的配置文件。

      有关更多详细信息，请参阅： https ://github.com/linuxserver/docker-code-server#parameters

      入门（适用于 linuxserver/code-server）

      您只需要运行容器，打开浏览器并转到 url http://localhost:8443 。

      我的用例

      我实现了一个系统，它允许通过一个带有微小界面和一些按钮的 WEB UI 创建、编辑、重新启动、删除代码服务器实例。 我准备了一个带有默认包和默认配置的实例，以便在内部公司网络中可用。

      我添加了一项功能，允许通过docker commit命令（例如：代码服务器模板）保存、恢复、复制和共享带有扩展、代码和包的整个实例。 我还添加了自定义 docker 镜像（如 Jupyter、Django、React 等）的自定义 mod。

      我使用Django + Ansible + Nginx + Docker的组合来实现系统（代码服务器生成器）。 多亏了 Nginx 和一个可叠加的 docker swarm 网络，我可以通过一个路由/端点访问所有实例。 如果您有兴趣，我可以提供有关整个堆栈的更多详细信息。

      结论

      这个概念允许使用 VS Code 和所需的层/包获得现成的开发人员容器，从而改善开发人员体验。

      对于企业用途，它允许部署一个自定义实例，该实例在企业网络（代理、防火墙等）内工作。 在我的例子中，我在一个强大的 worker 中部署了 100 个实例，从而使我们能够降低单台机器相对于 worker 的成本。 它还允许直接使用代码服务器实例解决最常见的问题并缩短安装和开发应用程序所花费的时间。

      对于个人使用，它可以在本地或远程部署，并用于拥有一个带有点文件和所需层的容器。 但实际上，如果使用多个实例，则更相关。

      还有其他用例：

      • 快速部署（节省安装时间）

      • 临时使用（实习生）

      • 一个事件（例如：Hackaton - 生成多个代码服务器）

      • 改善开发者环境

      • 测试代码服务器

      而你，你觉得这个解决方案怎么样？

      链接



      原文:

      I will introduce the concept of code-server.

      I am neither the creator nor the developer of the code-server.

      Genesis

      I ran into corporate network issues due to some limitation (proxy, firewall, powerless machine, mastered OS) since I found the opensource code-server repositories.

      Concept

      Coder release

      Run VS Code on any machine anywhere and access it in the browser.

      Highlights

      • Code on any device with a consistent development environment
      • Use cloud servers to speed up tests, compilations, downloads, and more
      • Preserve battery life when you're on the go; all intensive tasks run on your server

      Requirements

      See requirements for minimum specs, as well as instructions on how to set up a Google VM on which you can install code-server.

      TL;DR: Linux machine with WebSockets enabled, 1 GB RAM, and 2 vCPUs

      Getting started

      There are four ways to get started:

      If you use the install script, you can preview what occurs…

      Actually, Code-server is VS Code running on a remote server, accessible through the browser.

      Here are the main advantages :

      • Access through Web UI

      • Code on any device with a consistent development environment.

      • All intensive computation runs on your server.

      • Preserve battery life when you're on the go.

      • You're no longer running excess instances of Chrome.

      • Easy collaboration

      You can take a tour to the coder website which offers a free version and an enterprise extended version with 24/7h support and provides cloud server auto-deployment (Coder).

      Linuxserver release

      The LinuxServer.io team brings you another container release featuring:

      • regular and timely application updates
      • easy user mappings (PGID, PUID)
      • custom base image with s6 overlay
      • weekly base OS updates with common layers across the entire LinuxServer.io ecosystem to minimise space usage, down time and bandwidth
      • regular security updates

      Find us at:

      • Blog - all the things you can do with our containers including How-To guides, opinions and much more!
      • Discord - realtime support / chat with the community and the team.
      • Discourse - post on our community forum.
      • Fleet - an online web interface which displays all of our maintained images.
      • GitHub - view the source for all of our repositories.
      • Open Collective - please consider helping us by either donating or contributing to our budget

      Code-server is VS Code running on a remote server, accessible through the browser.

      • Code on your Chromebook, tablet, and laptop with a consistent…

      The LinuxServer.io team brings a container release linuxserver/code-server. It offers more flexibility and customization than the coder release. It embeds a VS code with some layers (Docker, Python, NodeJS, Java, PHP, etc ... -> docker-mods) and some default packages.

      Here are the main features :

      • Custom base docker image with S6 overlay (for more details : just-containers/s6-overlay)

      • Full customisation and docker layer mods integration (linuxserver/docker-mods)

      • Weekly base OS updates and regular security updates

      • Easy authentication setup

      • Domain proxying

      • etc ...

      Here is the list of layers : https://mods.linuxserver.io/?mod=code-server.

      There is also other version/repository of the code-server.

      Requirements (for linuxserver/code-server)

      • Docker
      • 1 GB of RAM minimum (2 or 4 GB of RAM is better)
      • 2 CPU cores minimum

      See for more details : https://github.com/coder/code-server/blob/main/docs/requirements.md

      Installation (for linuxserver/code-server)

      Here is a sample of the docker-compose.yml then you get started creating the container.

      ---
version: "2.1"
services: code-server: image: linuxserver/code-server:latest container_name: code-server environment: - PUID=1000 - PGID=1000 - TZ=Europe/London - PASSWORD=password #optional - HASHED_PASSWORD= #optional - SUDO_PASSWORD=password #optional - SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH= #optional - PROXY_DOMAIN=code-server.my.domain #optional - DEFAULT_WORKSPACE=/config/workspace #optional - DOCKER_MODS=linuxserver/mods:python #example volumes: - /path/to/appdata/config:/config ports: - 8443:8443 restart: unless-stopped
      Parameter Function
      -p 8443 web gui
      -e PUID=1000 for UserID - see below for explanation
      -e PGID=1000 for GroupID - see below for explanation
      -e TZ=Europe/London Specify a timezone to use EG Europe/London
      -e PASSWORD=password Optional web gui password, if PASSWORD or HASHED_PASSWORD is not provided, there will be no auth.
      -e HASHED_PASSWORD= Optional web gui password, overrides PASSWORD, instructions on how to create it is below.
      -e SUDO_PASSWORD=password If this optional variable is set, user will have sudo access in the code-server terminal with the specified password.
      -e SUDO_PASSWORD_HASH= Optionally set sudo password via hash (takes priority over SUDO_PASSWORD var). Format is $type$salt$hashed.
      -e PROXY_DOMAIN=code-server.my.domain If this optional variable is set, this domain will be proxied for subdomain proxying. See Documentation
      -e DEFAULT_WORKSPACE=/config/workspace If this optional variable is set, code-server will open this directory by default
      -v /config Contains all relevant configuration files.

      See for more details : https://github.com/linuxserver/docker-code-server#parameters

      Getting started (for linuxserver/code-server)

      You just have to run the container, open your browser and go to the url http://localhost:8443 .

      My use case

      I implemented a system which allows to create, edit, restart, delete a code-server instance through a WEB UI with a tiny interface and some buttons. I prepared an instance with default packages and default configuration in order to be usable in the internal corporate network.

      I added a feature that allows to save, restore, duplicate and share the entire instance with extensions, code and packages through the docker commit command (ex: code-server templates). I also added custom mods which are custom docker images (like Jupyter, Django, React, etc ...).

      I used the combination of Django + Ansible + Nginx + Docker for the implementation of the system (code-server generator). Thanks to Nginx with an overlay attachable docker swarm network, I can reach all instances through one route/endpoint. If you are interested, I can give more details about the entire stack.

      Conclusion

      This concept allows to get a readymade developer container with VS Code and desired layers/packages improving the developer experience.

      For corporate usage, it allows to deploy a custom instance, which works inside the corporate network (proxy, firewall, etc ...). In my case, I deployed 100 instances in one powerful worker allowing us to reduce the cost of the single machines vs the worker. It also permits to solve most common problem and improving the time spent for the installation and the development of an application directly with the code-server instance.

      For personal usage, it could be deployed locally or remotely and be used to have a single container with dotfiles and needed layers. But in fact, it is more relevant if multiple instances are used.

      There are other use cases :

      • a fast deploy (for saving installation time)

      • a temporary usage (for an intern)

      • an event (ex: Hackaton - generate multiple code-server)

      • improve the developer environment

      • testing the code-server

      And you, what do you think about this solution ?

      Dev.to Posts

      Links

SvelteKit is in its release candidate phase now. The recent breaking changes get SvelteKit to a new level.

But at the same time this changes made almost every older SvelteKit tutorial rather obsolete, including the ones I have listed in my older posts (The Best SvelteKit YouTube Tutorials – UPDATED on Feb 2022 and The Best SvelteKit YouTube Tutorials).
